paper_identity.title: ACORN: Performant and Predicate-Agnostic Search Over Vector Embeddings and Structured Data

scientific_problem.problem: Performance degradation and strict semantic limitations in hybrid vector-relational search under medium-high filter selectivities or negative query correlations.
scientific_problem.motivation: Modern multimodal applications need flexible, expressive, and unbounded predicates in runtime without causing exponential index growth or linear scan bottlenecks.
scientific_problem.gap: Existing methods either linearize search (pre-filtering), cause massive graph exploration loops (post-filtering), or lack general predicate expressiveness (specialized hardware/indices like Filtered-DiskANN).
scientific_problem.hypothesis: Dense structured neighborhoods generated during index time or expanded dynamically during query time can maintain local connectivity within arbitrary induced predicate subgraphs, emulating an ideal partitioned oracle.
scientific_problem.research_question: How to design a predicate-agnostic vector graph index that handles complex, runtime-defined structured filters with sublinear query latency?
scientific_problem.success_criteria: Achieving state-of-the-art throughput (QPS) at 0.9 fixed recall across standard benchmarks and large-scale synthetic workloads.

methods.name: ACORN-γ
methods.role: High-efficiency query index variant with offline dense graph construction
methods.input: Vectors, structured attributes, parameters (M, efc, gamma, M_beta)
methods.output: Densified and pruned HNSW graph structure
methods.claimed_advantage: Up to 1,000x higher QPS at 0.9 recall, robust to negative query correlation.
methods.limitation: Higher indexing time (TTI) and memory footprint on uncompressed layers.

methods.name: ACORN-1
methods.role: Resource-constrained index variant with query-time graph densification
methods.input: Vectors, structured attributes, standard HNSW configuration
methods.output: Standard HNSW graph structure traversed with a 2-hop search logic
methods.claimed_advantage: Low indexing overhead matching HNSW baseline, highly reduced RAM fingerprint.
methods.limitation: Averages 1.5x to 5x lower peak QPS compared to ACORN-gamma.

datasets.name: TripClick
datasets.usage: High Cardinality Predicate Set (HCPS) Real C workloads

metrics.name: Recall@10
metrics.definition: Intersection of ground-truth and retrieved set divided by 10

metrics.name: Queries Per Second (QPS)
metrics.definition: Total successful queries processed per unit time
metrics.measures: System throughput and raw latency response
